[JS Weird World] No.64 Building Safe, Reusable Code
Now that we've got the requirements sorted out, it's time to make this thing safe to reuse in any JavaScript environment.
Our starting setup looks like this — just four files. We need jQuery support, so that has to load first, and then we load our own little framework after it.
Most of our work happens in

The first thing we need to do is create a new execution scope. That way the variables we write stay safe and don't leak out into the global scope. So we'll use an IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression) here, and this IIFE needs to be able to grab the global
And that's our most basic safe environment ready to go.

Now that we've got the requirements sorted out, it's time to make our little framework safe to reuse in any JavaScript environment. In this post we wrap everything in an IIFE and hand it the global window and jQuery objects, so our variables stay protected and don't leak out.
